zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Grace Watkins於1650年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州時，她的父親James Watkins22歲，母親Elizabeth Baker18歲。她於大約1667年與George Richman在美國維吉尼亞州結婚。她去世於1737年3月8日在美國維吉尼亞州素利素利享年87歲，埋葬在英國薩默塞特度爾維頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Grace Watkins于1650年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州时，她的父亲James Watkins22岁，母亲Elizabeth Baker18岁。她于大约1667年与George Richman在美国弗吉尼亚州结婚。她去世于1737年3月8日在美国弗吉尼亚州素利素利享年87岁，埋葬在英国薩默塞特度爾維頓。

nameMeanings.name: Watkins
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Welsh: variant of Watkin , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Watkin
meanings: English and Welsh: from the personal name Watkin, a diminutive of Walter .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Grace
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the abstract noun (via Old French, from Latin gratia), this name occurs occasionally in the 15th century, and by the 1540s was among the most popular girls' names in some parishes. It has always been particularly popular in Scotland and northern England (borne, for example, by Grace Darling , the lighthouse keeper's daughter whose heroism in 1838 , saving sailors in a storm, caught the popular imagination). In more recent times it was famous as the name of the actress Grace Kelly ( 1928–82 ), who became Princess Grace of Monaco. In Ireland it is used as an Anglicized form of Gráinne .

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

historicEvents.title: South Sea Bubble
historicEvents.description: The South Sea Bubble Bill was passed by the House of Lords in 1720. This allowed the South Sea company to monopolize trade with South America. The company underwrote the English National Debt which promised 5% interest from the government. As shares rose exponentially, many companies were created and many fortunes were made. The stocks crashed and many people lost their money which caused them to become destitute overnight and suicide was common.  Robert Walpole took charge of the South Sea Bubble Financial Crisis by dividing the national debt between the Bank of England, the Treasury, and the Sinking Fund.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Grace Watkins was born in 1650, in Virginia, British Colonial America, her father, James Watkins, was 22 and her mother, Elizabeth Baker, was 18. She married George Richman about 1667, in Virginia, United States. She died on 8 March 1737, in Surry, Surry, Virginia, United States, at the age of 87, and was buried in Dulverton, Somerset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Grace Watkins est née en 1650, à Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, James Watkins, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Baker, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé George Richman environ 1667, à Virginie, États-Unis. Elle est décédée le 8 mars 1737, à Surry, Surry, Virginie, États-Unis, à l’âge de 87 ans, et a été enterrée à Dulverton, Somerset, Angleterre.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Grace Watkins è nata il nel 1650 nel Virginia, British Colonial America, sua padre, James Watkins, aveva 22 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Baker, aveva 18 anni. Lei sposato George Richman circa 1667, nel Virginia, Stati Uniti d'America. Morì il 8 marzo 1737, nel Surry, Surry, Virginia, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 87 anni, e fu sepolta a Dulverton, Somerset, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Grace Watkins(이)가 1650년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 James Watkins(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Baker(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 대략 1667년 미국 버지니아 주에서 George Richman(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1737년 3월 8일 미국 버지니아 주 수리 수리에서 87세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 서머세트 드버턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Grace Watkins nasceu em 1650, em Virginia, British Colonial America, seu pai, James Watkins, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Baker, tinha 18 anos. Ela casou-se com George Richman aproximadamente 1667, em Virgínia, Estados Unidos da América. Ela faleceu em 8 de março de 1737, em Surry, Surry, Virgínia, Estados Unidos da América, com 87 anos, e foi sepultada em Dulverton, Somerset, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Grace Watkins родилась в 1650 г. в местности Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика, её отцу по имени James Watkins было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Baker, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени George Richman около 1667 г. в местности Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты. Она умерла 8 марта 1737 г. в местности сурри, сурри, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 87 лет и была похоронена в местности дулвиртон, сомирсет, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Grace Watkins nació en 1650, en Virginia, British Colonial America, su papá, James Watkins, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Baker, tenía 18 años. Se casó con George Richman aproximadamente 1667, en Virginia, Estados Unidos. Murió el 8 de marzo de 1737, en Surry, Surry, Virginia, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 87 años, y fue sepultada en Dulverton, Somerset, Inglaterra.
